items.headline: City Council approves Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a Seeds of Peace Monument artwork by artist Mario Chiodo, to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This public art installation memorializes peace efforts in the community.

items.headline: City Council adjusts district office budgets during transition years
items.summary: The City Council proposed changes to City Council Policy 1-18, Section 20, governing how Office of the Mayor and City Council District Office budgets are handled in transition years. This affects how council members' district funds are allocated during election or leadership transition periods.

items.headline: City Council Appoints New Permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills a key leadership position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementation of council policy.

items.headline: City releases draft Urban Water Master Plan and shortage contingency strategy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plans address the city's water management and preparedness for drought conditions, which directly affect local water rates and availability for residents and businesses.

items.headline: The Core Companies permitted for $1.9M office renovation on Santa Clara
items.summary: A $1.94M tenant improvement permit has been issued for 333 W Santa Clara St in downtown San José. The project reflects ongoing demand for renovated office space in the city's central business district.

items.headline: Analog Devices permitted for $2.25M office renovation downtown
items.summary: The semiconductor giant has received a permit for a major tenant improvement at 160 Rio Robles, signaling continued investment in San José's tech corridor. The $2.25M project represents significant corporate commitment to the city's office market.

items.headline: City updates rules for accessory dwelling units to match state law
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its municipal code to align accessory dwelling unit (ADU) and junior accessory dwelling unit (JADU) regulations with recent changes in state law. This affects what homeowners can build on their properties and permitting requirements.

items.headline: Stream corridor protection rules under review
items.summary: The city is considering amendments to stream corridor protection ordinances. The specifics of what protections are being changed were not detailed in the agenda.
